description: Corrective migration adds activity_type, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by columns to rmm_alerts
Migration adds dedup_key to rmm_alerts with index on (tenant, integration_id, dedup_key)
Migration adds occurrence_count (default 1) and last_occurrence_at to rmm_alerts
Migration adds nullable matched_rule_id to rmm_alerts
Migration adds auto_ticket_created (default false) to rmm_alerts
Migration adds conditions and actions jsonb (default {}) to rmm_alert_rules
Migration drops the eleven flat filter/action columns from rmm_alert_rules
Migration down() restores the prior schema
NormalizedRmmAlertEvent contract defined in shared/rmm/alerts/contracts.ts with kind triggered|reset|acknowledged
RmmAlertOutboundAdapter interface with per-provider resolution; providers without an adapter are skipped
Shared Zod schemas for rule conditions and actions JSONB shapes
mapNinjaOneWebhookToAlertEvent normalizer: field mapping, severity normalization, condition identity from statusCode falling back to activityType
TacticalRMM payload normalizer producing NormalizedRmmAlertEvent
NinjaOne webhook routes CONDITION TRIGGERED/RESET/ACKNOWLEDGED through processRmmAlertEvent, replacing inline rmm_alerts writes; auth/tenant-resolution/tier gating unchanged
Tactical webhook routes alert payloads through processRmmAlertEvent, replacing its direct rmm_alerts writes
processRmmAlertEvent entry point dispatches by event kind
Triggered events upsert rmm_alerts on (tenant, integration_id, external_alert_id) with raw payload stored in metadata jsonb
dedup_key computed from device + condition identity and stored on the alert row
Rule evaluation selects active rules for the integration ordered by priority_order; first match wins
All present condition fields must match: severities, activityTypes, alertClasses, sourceTypes, organizationIds, keywords
messagePattern regex condition evaluated; a rule that fails to evaluate is logged and skipped without aborting the pipeline
A rule with an empty conditions object matches every alert (catch-all)
matched_rule_id persisted on the alert row for later lifecycle decisions
No matching rule, or matched rule with createTicket false, stores the alert without creating a ticket
Dedup: alert whose dedup_key matches an alert with a still-open linked ticket joins that ticket and increments occurrence_count / last_occurrence_at
Dedup repeat adds an internal 're-triggered — Nth occurrence' comment to the existing ticket
No open dedup ticket: a new ticket is created via the shared alert ticket creator
Ticket creation honors rule actions boardId, priorityOverride, and assignToUserId
Severity-to-priority fallback mapping applies when no priorityOverride is set
Ticket templates render {{device}}, {{message}}, {{severity}}, {{organization}} placeholders, with sensible defaults when no template is set
Created tickets get source + source_reference, an asset association, and an initial internal comment with alert details
Client resolution for the ticket: the asset's client, else the organization mapping's client
auto_ticket_created set and ticket_id stored on the alert row after ticket creation
Reset events mark the alert resolved and set resolved_at
Reset with a linked ticket and rule autoResolveTicket always adds a resolution comment; without the flag the ticket is untouched
Untouched determination: no human-authored comments, no time entries, no manual status change; rule auto-assignment does not count
Untouched tickets close via actions.autoResolveStatusId, else the tenant's first is_closed status
Touched tickets stay open after alert reset (comment only)
Acknowledged events set alert status acknowledged with acknowledged_at/acknowledged_by
Ticket-closed event-bus subscriber finds unresolved linked alerts by ticket_id and honors the matched rule's resetAlertOnTicketClose (default true)
NinjaOne outbound adapter resets the alert via NinjaOneClient.resetAlert and updates the local alert status
Outbound reset failures are logged and stamped into alert metadata and never block the ticket close
Tactical outbound adapter implemented if its API supports alert resolution; otherwise the provider ships without an adapter and the pipeline skips outbound reset
listRmmAlertRules server action returns rules for an integration ordered by priority_order
createRmmAlertRule server action validates conditions/actions against the shared Zod schemas
updateRmmAlertRule server action
deleteRmmAlertRule server action
reorderRmmAlertRules server action updates priority_order
All rule CRUD actions are admin-gated and tenant-scoped
Save-time validation rejects an invalid messagePattern regex
Alert Rules section renders in RMM integration settings for both NinjaOne and TacticalRMM
Rules list is priority-ordered with active toggle, reorder controls, edit and delete
Rule editor Match group: severities, activity types, alert classes, source types, keywords, message regex
Rule editor organization picker fed from rmm_organization_mappings
Rule editor Actions group: create-ticket toggle, board picker, priority override, assignee picker
Rule editor title/description template inputs with placeholder hints
Rule editor autoResolveTicket and resetAlertOnTicketClose toggles and notify-users picker
Rule editor surfaces validation errors inline; delete requires confirmation
RMM_ALERT_TRIGGERED registered in the workflow v2 event catalog with a provider-generic payload schema
RMM_ALERT_RESOLVED registered in the workflow v2 event catalog
Pipeline publishes the workflow v2 alert events, replacing the orphaned legacy-bus publishes
rmm.alerts.create_ticket workflow action invokes the shared ticket creator by alert ID
rmm-alert notification category registered and honoring per-user notification preferences
Matched rule notifyUserIds produce in-app notifications
Matched rule notifyUserIds produce email notifications per user preference
CSRF validation implemented in the NinjaOne OAuth callback using the state payload's csrf and timestamp
ninjaone/alerts modules moved into shared/rmm/alerts with imports updated and the superseded resetInNinjaOne TODO removed
rmm_organization_mappings.auto_create_tickets deprecated: no read paths remain; rules are the sole gate for ticket creation
